Off Calendar Feb-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Thomas A. Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for Intervenor, Hartford Accident and Indemnity Company, et al;
Mariam Pambukyan, Esq. of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendant;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The matter is called and not contested. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The July 8, 2024 trial date is advanced to June 3, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 12, 2024.
b. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 28, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. The Court will allow eight summary judgment/adjudication motions to be calendared in Asbestos Law and Motion for this action, unless the Court finds good cause for additional summary judgment/adjudication motions to be filed.
c. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.
3. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 17, 2024.
6. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 31, 2024.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 13, 2024.
a. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
8. No later than February 27, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each defendant.
b. Defendants must also provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini minutes continues in Part 2 of 2).
